content.problemStatement:
Given an array of integers representing heights of bars in a histogram, find the area of the largest rectangle that can be formed by these bars. Each bar has width 1.


Input Format

First line contains T, number of test cases
Each test case contains two lines of input

First line contains N, number of bars
Second line contains N space-separated integers representing heights of bars




Output Format

For each test case, print the area of the largest possible rectangle.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the number of bars in the histogram; the second line contains N space-separated integers h₁, h₂, …, h_N representing the heights of the bars.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the area of the largest possible rectangle that can be formed using consecutive bars of the histogram.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: the maximal rectangle uses bars 3 and 4 (heights 5 and 6) with height 5 and width 2, area 10. Test case 2: all bars have height 2, width 4, area 8.
Test case 1: rectangle of height 1 covering all three bars gives area 3. Test case 2: rectangle of height 3 covering bars 3,4,5 gives area 9.
All bars have height 1, width 5, so the largest rectangle area is 5.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using a stack-based approach. We iterate through the histogram bars and push the indices of the bars onto the stack. If the current bar is smaller than the bar at the top of the stack, we calculate the area of the rectangle that can be formed with the bar at the top of the stack as the smallest bar. We keep track of the maximum area found so far.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T * N) total. The precomputation is done once for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the stack.

code.Python3:
import sys

def largest_rectangle(heights):
    stack = [-1]
    max_area = 0
    for i, h in enumerate(heights):
        while len(stack) > 1 and h < heights[stack[-1]]:
            height = heights[stack.pop()]
            width = i - stack[-1] - 1
            max_area = max(max_area, height * width)
        stack.append(i)
    while len(stack) > 1:
        height = heights[stack.pop()]
        width = len(heights) - stack[-1] - 1
        max_area = max(max_area, height * width)
    return max_area

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        heights = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        out_lines.append(str(largest_rectangle(heights)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
